name: serac-claude-code
description: Manage your Claude Code agents, workflows, and teams from the VS Code sidebar: status cards, drill-in detail panel, usage quotas, and transcripts.

serac.show.foreignWorkspaces.description: Show the "Other workspaces" section. When off, the section is hidden and discovery scanning is skipped.

serac.show.worktrees.description: Show the "Worktrees" section. When off, the section is hidden and worktree scanning is skipped.

serac.show.usage.description: Show the usage / quota section at the bottom of the panel.

serac.show.subagents.description: Show subagent rows inside session cards.

serac.show.fileCollisions.description: Show a 'N shared files' chip when two ACTIVE sessions have edited the same file (a lost-work hazard when running multiple agents in one checkout). Off by default — rarely fires when parallelising via worktrees.

serac.show.workflows.description: Surface Claude Code Workflow runs on their session cards (tag, roll-up, and detail panel).

serac.animations.enabled.description: Enable card-slide and FLIP animations. Disable for snappier rendering or to respect reduced-motion preferences.

serac.foreignWorkspaces.maxHeightPx.description: Maximum pixel height of the "Other workspaces" pane before it scrolls internally. Rows are ~18px, so 280 ≈ 15 rows. Set to 0 for auto (no cap).

serac.worktrees.maxHeightPx.description: Maximum pixel height of the "Worktrees" pane before it scrolls internally. Rows are ~18px, so 280 ≈ 15 rows. Set to 0 for auto (no cap).

serac.worktrees.autoCollapseAfterSeconds.description: Auto-collapse the worktree picker after this many seconds of idle.

serac.worktrees.consolidateTmp.description: Consolidate worktree display: group scratch sessions under /private/tmp (and /tmp) into a single "tmp" pseudo-repository row in "Other workspaces", instead of one flat row per directory. Expand the row to pick a specific directory.

serac.discovery.ageGateDays.description: Base staleness window: ignore foreign workspaces, worktrees, agent teams, and workflows whose latest activity is older than this many days. Each section can override this below; leave an override empty to inherit this value.

enumDescriptions: Only sessions whose Claude Code process is live right now (session-only). Falls back to the day-count window if the process registry can't answer.
Sessions active in the last day.
Sessions active in the last 7 days.
Sessions active in the last 30 days.
serac.discovery.foreignWorkspacesWindow.description: Visibility preset for the "Other workspaces" section. Overrides the day-count settings unless set to "inherit".

serac.discovery.foreignWorkspacesAgeGate.description: Staleness window (days) for the "Other workspaces" section only. Leave empty to inherit serac.discovery.ageGateDays.

serac.discovery.worktreesAgeGateDays.description: Staleness window (days) for the worktrees shown inline in the main list. Leave empty to inherit serac.discovery.ageGateDays.

serac.discovery.teamsAgeGateDays.description: Staleness window (days) for Agent Teams. Leave empty to inherit serac.discovery.ageGateDays.

serac.discovery.workflowsAgeGateDays.description: Staleness window (days) for Workflow runs. Leave empty to inherit serac.discovery.ageGateDays.

serac.refresh.intervalSeconds.description: Panel refresh interval in seconds. Lower = more responsive timestamps, higher = lower CPU.

serac.usage.warnAtPercent.description: Tint usage as 'warn' when burn rate reaches this percentage.

serac.usage.criticalAtPercent.description: Tint usage as 'critical' when burn rate reaches this percentage.

serac.sessions.highConfidenceSeconds.description: A running or waiting session's status is shown at high confidence while its last activity is younger than this many seconds. After this, the card dims to medium confidence. Lower = quicker to flag a possibly-stalled session.

serac.sessions.mediumConfidenceSeconds.description: A running or waiting session shows medium confidence until its last activity is older than this many seconds, then drops to low confidence. Should be greater than serac.sessions.highConfidenceSeconds.

serac.hooks.enabled.description: Register Serac's Claude Code hook forwarder in this workspace's .claude/settings.json. When enabled, permission-wait detection becomes hook-driven (sub-second) instead of heuristic (3-6 seconds). Each workspace opts in independently; disabling cleanly removes Serac-managed entries.

serac.hooks.debug.description: Log every inbound hook event to the 'Serac (Hooks)' output channel. Useful for debugging hook flow when serac.hooks.enabled is on. Off by default — leader-bound and liveness-warning lines log regardless.

serac.cleanup.confirmRequired.description: Require a second click to confirm the title-bar cleanup action (the arm-then-confirm dance).

serac.archive.defaultRange.description: Default time range for archived sessions. The bottom pills override this per-session.

serac.archive.maxDoneShown.description: Maximum number of completed (done) items shown in the archive when expanded.

serac.experimental.teammateMessaging.description: Experimental, off by default. Enables an inline composer at the foot of a live teammate's transcript that writes a message directly into that member's inbox — Serac's only write into ~/.claude/. Direct delivery bypasses the team lead and has no delivery guarantee (the member drains its inbox on a ~5s poll). Disable to remove the composer and the write path entirely.

serac.experimental.operatorName.description: The sender label written as the inbox entry's "from" when you message a teammate (e.g. your name). Used only when serac.experimental.teammateMessaging is on. Must be 1-100 chars of letters, digits, '-' or '_'.
